gold_transitions: Identify acute pleuritic chest pain plus hypoxemia/tachycardia
Recognize major VTE risks (recent prolonged immobility, estrogen exposure)
Use elevated D-dimer and nonfocal CXR to prioritize PE over pneumonia/musculoskeletal pain
accepted_probe_examples: Ask about leg swelling or prior DVT/PE
Ask about hemoptysis or recent surgery
Recommend CT pulmonary angiography or V/Q scan based on stability

gold_transitions: Link episodic nighttime cough/wheeze to variable airway obstruction
Use bronchodilator reversibility (>12% FEV1 improvement) as key discriminator
Account for atopic triggers and normal baseline imaging
accepted_probe_examples: Ask about exercise/allergen/cold-air triggers
Confirm symptom variability over time
Differentiate from GERD/post-viral cough/COPD by age and spirometry

gold_transitions: Recognize edema + heavy proteinuria + hypoalbuminemia triad
Incorporate hyperlipidemia as supportive nephrotic feature
Separate from nephritic syndrome by absence of significant hematuria
accepted_probe_examples: Ask for diabetic nephropathy history and timeline
Check for thrombosis risk and renal function trend
Ask about urine blood/casts to evaluate nephritic overlap

gold_transitions: Identify hyperglycemia with ketonemia and anion-gap metabolic acidosis
Use polyuria/polydipsia/weight loss to support insulin-deficient state
Recognize Kussmaul respirations as compensation for metabolic acidosis
accepted_probe_examples: Ask about infection, missed insulin, or new-onset diabetes
Check mental status and dehydration severity
Prioritize fluids, insulin protocol, and potassium monitoring

gold_transitions: Combine systemic fever/chills with flank pain and positive urinalysis
Use CVA tenderness to localize infection to upper tract
Distinguish from uncomplicated cystitis by constitutional illness
accepted_probe_examples: Ask about pregnancy or urinary obstruction
Request urine culture before antibiotics when feasible
Screen for stone/abscess signs if not improving

gold_transitions: Identify unilateral postoperative calf swelling/tenderness as classic DVT pattern
Use immobilization and recent surgery as high-probability risk factors
Treat non-compressible popliteal vein on Doppler as confirmatory
accepted_probe_examples: Ask about chest pain or dyspnea suggesting PE
Ask about bleeding history or recent hemorrhage
Document provoking factors and expected treatment duration context

gold_transitions: Integrate adrenergic symptoms (tremor, tachycardia, heat intolerance) with weight loss
Use suppressed TSH with elevated free T4 as biochemical confirmation
Leverage diffuse goiter and autoimmune family history toward Graves etiology
accepted_probe_examples: Ask for eye symptoms or thyroid-stimulating immunoglobulin testing
Screen for atrial fibrillation symptoms
Confirm no exogenous thyroid hormone use

gold_transitions: Recognize complete unilateral facial weakness including forehead as peripheral pattern
Use inability to close eye and taste change to localize CN VII involvement
Exclude stroke pattern by absence of limb deficits/aphasia and preserved other focal findings
accepted_probe_examples: Ask about ear vesicles or Lyme exposure
Check exact onset window for steroid benefit
Recommend lubricating drops and eye taping if incomplete closure

gold_transitions: Match severe epigastric pain radiating to back with vomiting pattern
Use lipase >3x ULN as major diagnostic criterion
Incorporate alcohol binge as plausible trigger while monitoring severity signs
accepted_probe_examples: Ask about gallstone history and triglycerides
Assess for hypotension, hypoxia, or organ dysfunction
Plan early fluids and reassessment for necrosis/pseudocyst if worsening

accepted_diagnosis_synonyms: Extrahepatic biliary obstruction likely from pancreatic cancer
Cholestatic jaundice due to distal biliary obstruction
gold_transitions: Identify cholestatic lab pattern with conjugated hyperbilirubinemia
Use painless jaundice, pale stool, dark urine, and ductal dilation to localize obstruction
Incorporate age/weight loss/smoking as malignancy-leaning context
accepted_probe_examples: Ask for pancreatic-protocol CT or MRCP
Assess for fever/RUQ pain suggesting cholangitis or stone
Document timeline of weight loss and anorexia

gold_transitions: Recognize new-onset heart failure signs in late pregnancy/postpartum window
Use reduced LVEF with congestion findings to confirm systolic dysfunction
Exclude pre-existing structural disease based on history context
accepted_probe_examples: Ask about resting dyspnea, edema progression, and syncope
Screen for LV thrombus risk when EF severely reduced
Plan guideline-based HF therapy adjusted for breastfeeding status

gold_transitions: Identify abrupt monoarthritis of first MTP as classic podagra presentation
Use crystal-proven negatively birefringent needle-shaped urate findings as definitive
Treat hyperuricemia, thiazide use, and alcohol as supporting contributors
accepted_probe_examples: Ask about fever, immunosuppression, or bacteremia risk
Review diuretic exposure and alcohol/purine intake
Assess flare frequency and tophus history for long-term management planning
